answer:
Verdict: unit economics fundable, churn is the red flag.

- Gross margin/mo: $120×0.8 = $96
- LTV: $96/0.03 = $3,200
- LTV:CAC = 4:1 (benchmark ≥3, good)
- CAC payback: $800/$96 ≈ 8.3 mo (benchmark <12, good)

But 3% monthly logo churn = ~31%/yr — high, typical SMB. VCs will flag: churn caps scale (growth ceiling when new logos ≈ churned logos), implies weak retention/PMF. NRR matters more than logo churn — if expansion revenue pushes NRR ≥100%, story works; if NRR <90%, hard pass from most Series A investors.

Actions: report NRR + cohort curves in deck; if NRR <100%, fix retention/expansion before raising. Seed fundable now, Series A needs churn ≤2%/mo or NRR ≥100%.
